mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.3pp increase in the unemployment rate over 3 months signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in weakening labor market periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index over 2 months signals an increase in demand that boosts corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in increasing demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% decrease in the Industrial Production Index over 2 months signals a decrease in supply that reduces corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in decreasing supply periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the federal funds rate and a 1% decrease in the Consumer Price Index over 2 months signal a tightening monetary cycle that reduces demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.5% and CPIAUCSL falls < 1% in 2 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in tightening monetary cycle periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.2pp decrease in the unemployment rate and a 1% increase in the Consumer Price Index over 2 months signal a strengthening labor market that boosts demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls < 0.2pp and CPIAUCSL rises > 1% in 2 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in strengthening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the Consumer Price Index and a 0.5% increase in the federal funds rate over 2 months signal an increase in demand that boosts corporate earnings, but also a tightening monetary cycle that reduces demand, producing mixed S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 1% and FEDFUNDS rises > 0.5% in 2 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in mixed demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% decrease in the Industrial Production Index and a 0.2pp increase in the unemployment rate over 2 months signal a decrease in supply that reduces corporate earnings, but also a weakening labor market that reduces demand, producing mixed S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls < 1% and UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 2 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in mixed supply periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
